trade.summary: Bilateral trade reached record highs following Western sanctions on Russia post-2022. Cina exports electronics, vehicles, machinery, and consumer goods—filling gaps left by Western goods. Russia exports hydrocarbons (oil, gas, coal), minerals, and agricultural products. Trade has grown dramatically since 2022.
disputes: Chinese concerns about overly Russia-dependent trade profile
Russian concern about becoming too economically dependent on Cina
Pricing disputes on energy exports (Russia sells at discounts)

diplomatic.summary: Cina and Russia declared a 'no limits' partnership in February 2022, days before Russia's invasion of Ucraina. This reflects deep strategic alignment against Stati Uniti-led order. Cina has not condemned Russian aggression but abstained on UN resolutions. Cina provides economic lifeline through trade and financial services. Xi-Putin meetings are frequent. Both oppose 'Western hegemony'. Relationship is asymmetric—Cina is the dominant partner.
recent_incidents: Cina abstained (not voted against) UN resolutions condemning Russian invasion
Cina provided dual-use goods supporting Russian military production
Cina mediated Russia-Ucraina peace discussions while maintaining Russia ties

military.summary: Not a formal alliance but deep military cooperation. Joint exercises (Vostok, Joint Sea). Cina has provided dual-use goods (semiconductors, optics, navigation equipment) that supported Russian military. Russia has transferred advanced military technology to Cina (Su-35 aircraft, S-400 systems). Both oppose Stati Uniti missile defense systems. Military cooperation predates Ucraina war and has deepened since.
incidents: Russia transferred S-400 systems to China (2018)
Joint naval exercises in Baltic and Pacific seas (recurring)
China's supply of dual-use components to Russia's defense industry (2022-2025)

financial.summary: Cina has become Russia's financial lifeline. Trade conducted in RMB and rubles, bypassing SWIFT. Chinese banks that are not under Western sanctions serve as conduits. Chinese companies have filled market gaps left by Western firms. Cina is now Russia's largest creditor through trade credit. Russian sovereign wealth invested in Chinese yuan assets.

energy.summary: Energy is the backbone of the relationship. Russia has pivoted away from European gas markets to Cina. Power of Siberia pipeline operational. Russia is Cina's largest oil supplier. Multiple additional pipeline projects under development. LNG exports from Russian Arctic projects to Cina. Cina benefits from discounted Russian energy prices.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation as an alternative to Western partnerships. Space cooperation (lunar exploration), nuclear technology (Hualong-1 reactors), aviation (CR929 wide-body aircraft though struggling). Joint research in materials, AI, and Arctic technologies. Replacing Western partnerships post-sanctions.
